questions: Q1: Does the CLI's conflicted file (tickets/index.ts) have active conflict markers?
Q2: Is the CLI's Library module implementation intact after the staging merge?

answers.answer: No conflict markers exist. The file is 150 lines with all 10 expected ticket subcommands (list, latest, get, create, update-description, rerun, continue, artifacts, artifact, bundle). The auto-merge resolved the conflict between 4 ticket commits and 1 staging commit.
evidence: Grep <<<<<<< in tickets/index.ts: No matches found
File contains switch statement with all 10 ticket subcommand cases
All expected imports present: list, latest, get, create, rerun, continue, artifacts, artifact, bundle, update-description
merge-conflicts.json: 4 ticket commits (5cfbd79, ca6c51b, 210d9fc, 758fce3) vs 1 staging commit (6a4215c)

answers.answer: Yes. The library module is entirely separate from tickets/index.ts — it lives in src/library/ with its own router (src/library/index.ts) registered in src/index.ts at lines 98-102. The conflict was isolated to the ticket dispatcher which has nothing to do with library commands. All 6 library command files, the utility, SKILL.md update, and dispatcher registration are intact.
evidence: merge-conflicts.json lists ONLY tickets/index.ts — not any library file
scout/reference-map.json confirms all 9 Phase 2b steps complete: 6 library files + utility + dispatcher + SKILL.md
skill-content/SKILL.md has Library section at lines 146-179

statement: CLI conflict file (tickets/index.ts) is clean — no markers, auto-merge preserved both sides. The file is the ticket subcommand dispatcher with all 10 expected commands. The library module (src/library/) is entirely separate and unaffected. No CLI code changes needed for conflict resolution.
